title: Recurring Cost Calculator (RCA)
description: Industrial-grade recurring cost analysis tool based on Lean, Six Sigma, and ISO 14000 standards. Calculates total recurring cost, identifies hidden loss drivers, and suggests improvement actions.

inputs.label: Labor Rate (per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Average hourly wage including burden for direct labor operators.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average hourly wage including burden for direct labor operators.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Cycle
inputs.businessContext: Standard time per production cycle (e.g., per unit or batch).

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard time per production cycle (e.g., per unit or batch).

inputs.label: Material Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct material cost per unit of output.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct material cost per unit of output.

inputs.label: Energy Cost per Cycle
inputs.businessContext: Electricity, gas, or other energy costs per production cycle.

businessContext_i18n.en: Electricity, gas, or other energy costs per production cycle.

inputs.label: Maintenance Cost per Cycle
inputs.businessContext: Average preventive and corrective maintenance cost allocated per cycle.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average preventive and corrective maintenance cost allocated per cycle.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage applied to direct costs to cover indirect expenses (ISO 14051).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage applied to direct costs to cover indirect expenses (ISO 14051).

inputs.label: Cycle Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Actual cycle time per unit; used for throughput and efficiency calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Actual cycle time per unit; used for throughput and efficiency calculations.

inputs.label: Defect Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of units that fail quality inspection (Six Sigma metric).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of units that fail quality inspection (Six Sigma metric).

rules.message: Cycle time and labor hours must be positive.
Defect rate must be between 0% and 100%.
Shift pattern must be one of: single, double, continuous.

thresholds.message: Labor cost per cycle exceeds $50. Consider process improvement (Lean).
Defect rate above 5% — Six Sigma DMAIC project recommended.
Energy cost per cycle > $10. Investigate energy efficiency (ISO 50001).
Overhead rate > 50%. Review indirect cost drivers (ABC analysis).

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
